title: UI mutations on IO thread: onRescan() called from Dispatchers.IO after applyFixes
description: `onFixAll()` launches a coroutine on `Dispatchers.IO` (line 154: `scope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)`). After the `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block (lines 156–169), execution returns to the IO thread and line 171 calls `onRescan()`. Inside `onRescan()`, lines 113–114 directly mutate Swing components: `rescanButton.isEnabled = false` and `statusLabel.text = "Scanning..."`. These UI mutations execute on the IO thread, violating IntelliJ's threading model and risking visual corruption or JVM-level assertion failures under the Event Dispatch Thread checker.
suggestedFix: Wrap the `onRescan()` call inside a `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block at line 171, or change `onRescan()` itself to not mutate UI state directly when called from a non-EDT context. Specifically: replace line 171 with `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { onRescan() }`. Alternatively refactor so the post-fix rescan always starts via `SwingUtilities.invokeLater { onRescan() }`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. In `onFixAll()` (line 154), the outer `scope.launch {}` runs on `Dispatchers.IO` (the scope is declared at line 55 as `CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.IO)`). The `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)` block (lines 156–169) correctly bounces UI mutations to EDT, but on exit it returns to the IO dispatcher. Line 171 then calls `onRescan()` directly on the IO thread. Inside `onRescan()`, lines 113–114 perform `rescanButton.isEnabled = false` and `statusLabel.text = "Scanning..."` as synchronous Swing mutations without any EDT dispatch guard. These are off-EDT Swing mutations, which violate IntelliJ's threading model and can trigger `IllegalStateException` from the EDT checker, or silently corrupt component state. The fix (wrapping the `onRescan()` call in `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT)`) does not break any passing tests and is straightforwardly correct — `onRescan()` itself immediately launches a new IO coroutine and marshals all subsequent UI updates back to EDT.
recommendedAction: Wrap the call at line 171 with `withContext(Dispatchers.EDT) { onRescan() }` so the initial Swing mutations in `onRescan()` lines 113–114 execute on the EDT.

title: Copyright fix silently no-ops for headers between lines 15–30 (scan vs fix window mismatch)
description: `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile` scans the first **30** lines to detect `YEAR_OUTDATED` (lines 111, 141 in `CopyrightFixService.kt`). However, `applyFixes` in `CopyrightFixCard` reads only the first **15** lines as `headerRegion` (line 221) before calling `updateYearInHeader`. For files where the copyright year appears between lines 16–30 (common for Apache/EUPL/GPL headers), `analyzeFile` correctly classifies them `YEAR_OUTDATED`, but `applyFixes` misses the year in the 15-line window, `updateYearInHeader` returns the region unchanged, and no document mutation is written. The UI reports "Fixed N file(s)" but the file is untouched — a silent correctness failure.
evidence:
CopyrightFixService.kt:141:        val headerRegion = content.lines().take(30).joinToString("\n")
CopyrightFixCard.kt:221:                            val headerRegion = original.lines().take(15).joinToString("\n")
suggestedFix: Change `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes` line 221 from `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` to match the scan window used in `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile`. Also update the corresponding line 225 `drop(15)` to `drop(30)`. Extract the magic constant `30` into a shared `const val HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30` in `CopyrightFixService` and reference it from both the service and the card.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile` (line 141) and `hasCopyrightHeader` (line 111) both scan the first 30 lines to classify files as `YEAR_OUTDATED`. `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes` (line 221) reads only `original.lines().take(15)` and reassembles with `original.lines().drop(15)` (line 226). For any file where the copyright year appears on lines 16–30 (common for Apache/EUPL/GPL/LGPL preambles), `analyzeFile` correctly returns `YEAR_OUTDATED`, but `applyFixes` passes a 15-line window to `updateYearInHeader`, which finds no year and returns the region unchanged. The document is not mutated, yet `statusLabel.text = "Fixed N file(s)"` is shown — a silent correctness failure. Verified by reading both files; the constant divergence is unambiguous at those exact lines. The fix (changing take(15)/drop(15) to take(30)/drop(30)) carries zero risk of breaking passing tests since existing `CopyrightFixServiceTest` tests operate on single-line or short headers entirely within the 15-line window.
recommendedAction: Change `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` at line 221 and `original.lines().drop(15)` to `original.lines().drop(30)` at line 226 in `CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes`. Extract the constant 30 into `CopyrightFixService.HEADER_SCAN_LINES` and reference it from both files.

title: CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes uses take(15) but CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile uses take(30) — silent no-op for headers in lines 16-30
description: In `applyFixes()` (line 221), the header region scanned for year replacement is `original.lines().take(15)`. However, `CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile()` (lines 111, 141) was intentionally raised to `take(30)` to detect long Apache/EUPL headers (audit finding handover:F-3, confirmed by test `analyzeFile scans 30 lines for year update`). This mismatch means: `analyzeFile` correctly marks a file `YEAR_OUTDATED` when the copyright year appears on lines 16-30, but `applyFixes` only reads lines 1-15 and calls `service.updateYearInHeader(headerRegion)` on that truncated region — which finds nothing to update, falls through to `if (rewritten == headerRegion) original`, and silently leaves the file unchanged. The Fix All button reports success but the year is not updated.
suggestedFix:
Change line 221 of `CopyrightFixCard.kt` from `val headerRegion = original.lines().take(15).joinToString("
")` to `val headerRegion = original.lines().take(30).joinToString("
")`. Also change line 227 from `val rest = original.lines().drop(15).joinToString("
")` to `drop(30)` to reassemble the full file correctly. Add a regression test in `CopyrightFixServiceTest` covering a year on line 20.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed mismatch. CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() at lines 111 and 141 both use `content.lines().take(30)` to detect and assess outdated years. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 reads `original.lines().take(15)` for the header region passed to `service.updateYearInHeader(...)`, then reassembles with `drop(15)` at line 226. For any file where the copyright year appears on lines 16-30, analyzeFile() correctly marks it YEAR_OUTDATED, but applyFixes() searches only lines 1-15, finds nothing to update, and the `if (rewritten == headerRegion) original` branch leaves the file unchanged. The UI reports success but the year is not updated — a silent no-op for long Apache/EUPL headers. The fix (change take/drop constants from 15 to 30) is surgical and cannot break correct behavior for files whose copyright is within the first 15 lines.
recommendedAction: Change CopyrightFixCard.kt line 221 from `take(15)` to `take(30)`, and line 226 from `drop(15)` to `drop(30)`. Add a regression test covering a copyright year on line 20.

title: TimeLogCard.setTicket() has zero call sites — Log Work permanently disabled
description: TimeLogCard declares a public `setTicket(ticketKey: String?)` method at line 140, but it is never called anywhere in the codebase. The field `activeTicketId` initializes to `""` and is never updated in production. `refreshLogButtonState()` checks `ticketOk = activeTicketId.isNotBlank()` which is always false, so the Log Work button is permanently disabled and the card always shows the empty state 'Select a ticket to log time.' HandoverPanel.applyState() (line 101) fans state only to `header.updateState(state)`, `checksTab.updateState(state)`, and `banner.setFailures(...)` — ActionsTab/TimeLogCard receive nothing. The 'todayWorkLogged' checklist dot can never turn green via the UI card.
suggestedFix: In HandoverPanel.applyState() (or by subscribing TimeLogCard to WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow), call `timeLog.setTicket(state.ticketId.takeIf { it.isNotBlank() })` after each state emission. Since HandoverPanel does not hold a reference to the inner TimeLogCard, either (a) expose a method on ActionsTab that delegates to TimeLogCard, or (b) wire ActionsTab to collect `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` directly in its own scope and call timeLog.setTicket() on changes.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. A full worktree grep finds `setTicket` defined at TimeLogCard.kt:140 with zero production callers. ActionsTab (the container) does not call it. HandoverPanel.applyState() (line 101-112) fans state only to `header.updateState(state)`, `checksTab.updateState(state)`, and `banner.setFailures(...)` — ActionsTab and TimeLogCard receive no ticket-change notifications. TimeLogCard does not subscribe to `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` internally; it relies entirely on `setTicket()` being called externally. Since `activeTicketId` is initialized to `""` (line 71) and never updated, `refreshLogButtonState()` always computes `ticketOk = false`, keeping the Log Work button permanently disabled. The card always shows the empty state label. The CLAUDE.md for :handover explicitly states TimeLogCard calls `JiraService.logWork` and flips `markWorkLogged()`, but that code path is unreachable via the UI because the precondition for enabling the button is never satisfied. The fix — having ActionsTab or TimeLogCard subscribe to `WorkflowContextService.activeTicketFlow` — is load-bearing functionality restoration, not harmful.
recommendedAction: Wire `TimeLogCard.setTicket()` by subscribing to `WorkflowContextService.getInstance(project).activeTicketFlow` inside `TimeLogCard.init` (preferred, self-contained) or by exposing a delegation method on `ActionsTab` and calling it from `HandoverPanel.applyState()`. The self-contained subscription in TimeLogCard is architecturally cleaner since the card already holds the project reference.

title: CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes reads only 15 header lines while CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile scans 30
description: CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() (CopyrightFixService.kt line 111) scans the first 30 lines for copyright headers. When it finds a YEAR_OUTDATED entry from lines 16–30, it stores the stale year expression. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 then reads only `original.lines().take(15)` to build its `headerRegion`. updateYearInHeader() finds no match in the 15-line region, so `rewritten == headerRegion` and the file is left unchanged. The UI reports a failed fix attempt but the status stays YEAR_OUTDATED indefinitely. The scan-window mismatch is the root cause.
suggestedFix: Change line 221 in CopyrightFixCard.kt from `original.lines().take(15)` to `original.lines().take(30)` to match the 30-line window used by CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile(). Also update the corresponding reassembly at line 224: change `.drop(15)` to `.drop(30)`. Consider extracting the constant (e.g., `HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30`) into CopyrightFixService.companion so both paths share it.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. CopyrightFixService.analyzeFile() at line 111 uses content.lines().take(30) to detect and classify a YEAR_OUTDATED header. CopyrightFixCard.applyFixes() at line 221 uses original.lines().take(15) as the headerRegion passed to updateYearInHeader(). When the year expression falls on lines 16–30, updateYearInHeader() finds no match in the 15-line region, returns the unchanged string, the rewritten == headerRegion guard at line 223 triggers, and the file is left unmodified. The fix (changing take(15)/drop(15) to take(30)/drop(30)) is correct and harmless. The CopyrightCellRenderer already imports JBLabel (line 13) so there are no import conflicts. Note the suggested constant extraction is optional but the core 15→30 change is the fix.
recommendedAction: Change CopyrightFixCard.kt line 221 from original.lines().take(15) to original.lines().take(30), and line 226 (the drop) from drop(15) to drop(30). Optionally extract a HEADER_SCAN_LINES = 30 constant into CopyrightFixService.companion.

title: Non-atomic read-modify-write on HandoverStateService._stateFlow lost under concurrent events and EDT mark calls
description: `handleEvent` reads `_stateFlow.value` into `current` (line 220), computes `next`, then writes `_stateFlow.value = next` (line 326). Concurrently, `markCopyrightFixed()` (331), `markJiraCommentPosted()` (336), `markJiraTransitioned()` (341–343), and `markWorkLogged()` (349) all do the same non-atomic pattern from the EDT. Since `handleEvent` runs on the coroutine collector thread and the `markX()` methods are called from EDT, concurrent invocations can overwrite each other. Specifically: if `handleEvent` reads `current` (with `copyrightFixed=true`), then `markCopyrightFixed()` writes `copyrightFixed=true` first, then `handleEvent` overwrites with `next` whose `copyrightFixed` reflects the value at the time of the earlier read — the `markCopyrightFixed()` write is silently clobbered, causing the checklist dot to revert to PENDING incorrectly. Note: `resetStatusSlices()` correctly uses `_stateFlow.update { }` (line 113) but the other methods do not.
evidence:
220:        val current = _stateFlow.value
326:        _stateFlow.value = next
---
331:        _stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(copyrightFixed = true)
349:        _stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(todayWorkLogged = true)
suggestedFix: Replace all non-atomic read-modify-write patterns with `_stateFlow.update { current -> current.copy(...) }`. In `handleEvent`, change: `val current = _stateFlow.value; val next = when(event) { ... }; _stateFlow.value = next` to `_stateFlow.update { current -> when(event) { ... } }`. Similarly change all `markX()` methods to use `_stateFlow.update { it.copy(field = value) }`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real pattern, severity downgraded from High to Medium. The non-atomic read-modify-write is real: `handleEvent` (line 220 read, line 326 write) and all four `markX()` methods (lines 331, 336, 341–343, 349) use `_stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(...)` instead of `_stateFlow.update { }`. A race window exists between the IO-dispatched `handleEvent` coroutine and EDT-dispatched `markX()` calls. However, in practice, the user-actioned slices (`copyrightFixed`, `todayWorkLogged`, `jiraTransitioned`) are only set once per ticket lifecycle and are ticket-level (not PR-level), so the clobbering window requires improbably tight timing between a build/quality event and a manual user action. The harm if it does occur is a checklist dot reverting to PENDING until the next event. `resetStatusSlices()` correctly uses `update {}` (line 113), confirming the author was aware of the atomic API. Applying the fix cannot break any existing test since all `markX` tests run sequentially with Dispatchers.Unconfined.
recommendedAction: Replace all non-atomic `_stateFlow.value = _stateFlow.value.copy(...)` patterns in `markX()` methods and the `handleEvent` tail-write with `_stateFlow.update { current -> current.copy(...) }` to match the correct pattern already used in `resetStatusSlices()`.

title: JiraPreviewPane liveResults collector job is leaked — never cancelled on Disposable.dispose()
description: `JiraPreviewPane.init` creates an isolated root `Job` (`val collectorJob = Job()`, line 90) and launches a `SharedFlow.collect` coroutine on `CoroutineScope(cs.coroutineContext + collectorJob)`. Because `collectorJob` has no parent (it is not a child of `cs`'s Job), it is never cancelled when either `cs` is cancelled or when `dispose()` is called (line 131). Every `JiraPreviewPane` instance that is constructed but later replaced (e.g., when a template is selected, triggering a preview pane re-construction, or when the tool window tab is closed and re-opened) leaves a permanently-running SharedFlow collector that keeps the pane object reachable and continues processing `liveResults` events for stale content.
suggestedFix: Store `collectorJob` as a field and cancel it in `dispose()`: `private val collectorJob = Job()` at class level, use it in `init` as before, and add `collectorJob.cancel()` to the `dispose()` method. Alternatively — since the intent is to avoid the collector being a child of `cs` — use `cs.launch` with a `Job()` stored as a field and cancelled on dispose.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. `collectorJob = Job()` at line 90 is a local variable scoped to the `init` block; it is never stored as a field and never cancelled in `dispose()` (lines 131–133). The comment at line 88 correctly explains the intent (avoid UncompletedCoroutinesError in runTest), but the consequence is that every `JiraPreviewPane` instance leaves a permanently-running `SharedFlow.collect` coroutine when the pane is replaced or the tool window closes. The `dispose()` body is a no-op. This is a resource leak: the stale pane remains reachable through the running coroutine and processes `liveResults` events forever. The fix (store `collectorJob` as a field and call `collectorJob.cancel()` in `dispose()`) is safe — `JiraPreviewPaneTest` constructs panes via `newPane(scope)` inside `runTest`, and since the test scope is cancelled at the end of runTest, the collectorJob (which uses `cs.coroutineContext`) would also be cancelled. Existing tests pass with or without the fix.
recommendedAction: Promote `collectorJob` to a private field (`private val collectorJob = Job()`), use it in `init` as already written, and add `collectorJob.cancel()` to the `dispose()` method.

title: HandoverAiSummaryCache.getOrCompute uses coroutineScope{} that blocks the caller until the LLM response arrives, defeating Deferred coalescing intent
description: `getOrCompute` wraps `cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { async { ... } }` inside `coroutineScope { }` (lines 165–182). `coroutineScope { }` suspends until **all its child coroutines** complete. Since the `async { ... }` block (the LLM call) is launched as a child of this `coroutineScope`, the scope does not return until the LLM call finishes. The returned `Deferred` is already completed at line 183 when `deferred.await()` is called. The class documentation says the Deferred coalescing allows concurrent callers to "share one in-flight compute" without blocking until `await()`, but because each caller's `coroutineScope { }` blocks until the LLM response arrives, the concurrency benefit is lost — every caller to `changeSummary()` / `ticketSummary()` will block at the `coroutineScope { }` boundary for the full LLM latency, even on a cache hit for the second-concurrent caller.
suggestedFix: Replace the `coroutineScope { }` wrapper with the service's own `cs` scope: `val deferred = cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { cs.async { ... } }`. This makes `async { }` a child of the persistent service scope rather than a transient `coroutineScope`, preserving the Deferred in the map across coroutine cancellations of individual callers. The existing `deferred.await()` + `cache.remove(key, deferred)` in the catch block (lines 183–191) then correctly handles cancellation cleanup.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real. The `coroutineScope { }` at line 165 launches `async { LLM call }` as its child coroutine. `coroutineScope` suspends until ALL its children complete, so the first caller's `coroutineScope {}` block does not return until the LLM call finishes — the returned `Deferred` is already completed at line 182. The `deferred.await()` at line 183 then returns immediately with the cached result. For truly concurrent callers, a second caller's `computeIfAbsent` lambda does not fire (existing Deferred returned), so its `coroutineScope {}` has no children and exits immediately, then `deferred.await()` correctly suspends on the in-progress Deferred from the first caller. Coalescing works in the sense that only one LLM call is made, but the documentation claim that "concurrent callers share one in-flight compute" without blocking is violated for the first caller, who always blocks the full LLM latency inside `coroutineScope {}` before `getOrCompute` returns. The fix (using `cs.async {}` directly without `coroutineScope {}`) correctly makes `getOrCompute` return the in-flight Deferred immediately, allowing the caller to do other work and await later. Existing `HandoverAiSummaryCacheTest` tests pass either way since they use sequential `changeSummary()` calls.
recommendedAction: Replace `coroutineScope { cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { async { ... } } }` with `cache.computeIfAbsent(key) { cs.async { ... } }` so the Deferred is parented to the service scope and `getOrCompute` returns the in-flight Deferred immediately without blocking the caller.

title: WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted has no production emit site — EventBus handler in HandoverStateService is dead code
description: HandoverStateService.handleEvent() at lines 319–321 handles WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted and flips jiraCommentPosted=true. However, no production code ever emits this event: JiraServiceImpl.addComment() (jira module) returns a ToolResult but does not call eventBus.emit(JiraCommentPosted(...)). A global search confirms the only emit sites are in tests. The 'Jira comment posted' checklist dot can only turn green via the direct markJiraCommentPosted() call in ShareTab.handlePostComment(). If a comment is posted via the agent's jira tool (JiraTool action add_comment), the HandoverState is never updated.
suggestedFix: Add an EventBus emission in JiraServiceImpl.addComment() on success: `eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(key, commentId))`. Since JiraServiceImpl already has access to EventBus, this is a one-line change. Alternatively, since the agent's JiraTool also calls addComment(), it could emit the event after a successful result. Either path closes the gap between the EventBus contract and the actual state-update path.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. JiraServiceImpl.addComment() (lines 315–358) returns a ToolResult but never calls eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(...)). The only production code that flips jiraCommentPosted=true is markJiraCommentPosted() called from ShareTab.handlePostComment(). The EventBus handler in HandoverStateService at line 319 is therefore dead in production — it can only be exercised via tests. If a user posts a Jira comment via the agent's jira add_comment tool, HandoverState.jiraCommentPosted stays false. The severity is Medium, not High, because the workaround is to use the Share tab's post-comment button rather than the agent tool, and the checklist dot still turns green through the markJiraCommentPosted() path. Fixing by emitting the event from JiraServiceImpl.addComment() is safe and correct.
recommendedAction: Add eventBus.emit(WorkflowEvent.JiraCommentPosted(key, result.data.id)) on the ApiResult.Success branch inside JiraServiceImpl.addComment(). JiraServiceImpl already holds an eventBus reference. Alternatively emit from the agent's JiraTool after a successful add_comment result.

title: PluginSettings.aiSummariesEnabled is surfaced in HandoverConfigurable but never consulted by HandoverAiSummaryCache
description: HandoverConfigurable exposes a checkbox 'Compute {ai.changeSummary} and {ai.ticketSummary}' bound to PluginSettings.State.aiSummariesEnabled. The setting is stored correctly. However, HandoverAiSummaryCache.changeSummary() and ticketSummary() never read this setting — they always attempt LLM generation whenever a generator EP is available. The toggle in Settings → Handover → AI summaries is therefore a dead control: disabling it has no effect on runtime behavior.
suggestedFix: In HandoverAiSummaryCache, inject PluginSettings (add to the DI constructor). At the top of changeSummary() and ticketSummary(), check `if (!settings.state.aiSummariesEnabled) return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("AI summaries disabled")`. The test constructor already accepts injected collaborators, so adding PluginSettings there is straightforward.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverConfigurable.kt lines 110–114 bind aiSummariesEnabled to a checkbox. HandoverAiSummaryCache.changeSummary() (line 113) and ticketSummary() (line 125) call getOrCompute() unconditionally without ever reading PluginSettings.state.aiSummariesEnabled. The toggle in Settings → Handover → AI summaries is a dead control with no runtime effect. The fix is straightforward: read the setting in changeSummary()/ticketSummary() and return unavailable early. The test constructor already accepts injected collaborators so adding PluginSettings does not break any existing tests.
recommendedAction: Inject PluginSettings into HandoverAiSummaryCache (DI constructor and test constructor). At the top of changeSummary() and ticketSummary(), add: if (!settings.state.aiSummariesEnabled) return HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable("AI summaries disabled").

title: PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG keys build.planKey, build.number, automation.url silently resolve to unavailable — missing resolver branches
description: HandoverConfigurable.PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG (core/src/.../HandoverConfigurable.kt line 185) lists 'build.planKey', 'build.number', and 'automation.url' as selectable chip keys. Users enabling these chips in Settings expect a value. HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve() (lines 70–123) only handles 'build.url' (as permanently unavailable) and has no cases for 'build.planKey', 'build.number', or 'automation.url'. All three fall to the else branch returning HandoverPlaceholderValue.unavailable('unknown placeholder'). The values ARE available: build.planKey and build.number come from HandoverState.buildStatus.planKey/buildNumber.
suggestedFix: Add three `when` branches to HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve(): `"build.planKey"` → `state.buildStatus?.planKey`; `"build.number"` → `state.buildStatus?.buildNumber?.toString()`; `"automation.url"` → `state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink`. Return HandoverPlaceholderValue.available(value) when non-null, or unavailable with a descriptive reason. Remove 'build.planKey', 'build.number', 'automation.url' from the catalog if they are genuinely unsupported.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverConfigurable.PLACEHOLDER_CATALOG (line 188) lists build.planKey, build.number, and automation.url as selectable chip keys. HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve() handles build.url (line 99, hardcoded unavailable), but has no when-branches for build.planKey, build.number, or automation.url — all three fall through to the else branch at line 121 returning 'unknown placeholder'. HandoverState does carry BuildSummary with planKey and buildNumber (lines 224–229 in HandoverStateService), so build.planKey and build.number are resolvable. automation.url could be sourced from state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink. The CLAUDE.md architecture audit (Phase 7 T-Handover-c) explicitly notes build.url is always unavailable but says nothing about the other build keys being intentionally unresolved. Fixing adds three when-branches with null-guarded available/unavailable returns, causing no regressions.
recommendedAction: Add when-branches in HandoverPlaceholderResolver.resolve(): build.planKey → state.buildStatus?.planKey; build.number → state.buildStatus?.buildNumber?.toString(); automation.url → state.suiteResults.lastOrNull()?.bambooLink. Return available(value) when non-null, unavailable with a descriptive reason otherwise.

title: handleCopyWiki emits COPY_EMAIL action instead of a wiki-specific action, corrupting override audit log
description: `handleCopyWiki()` (line 159–166) emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL` at line 160 when the user copies the Jira wiki-format text to clipboard. The correct semantic is that the user performed a "Copy wiki" action (distinct from copying formatted HTML email). `HandoverOverrideTracker` persists every `HandoverOverride` event with its action type; since `COPY_WIKI` does not exist in the `HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`), the developer picked the wrong value. This means the override audit log misidentifies wiki copies as email copies, making the 30-day count in Settings unreliable for distinguishing which handover paths the user actually used.
suggestedFix: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in `WorkflowEvent.kt` (next to `COPY_EMAIL`). Then change line 160 in `ShareTab.kt` to `emitOverrideIfNeeded(WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI)`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real but severity downgraded from Medium to Low. `handleCopyWiki()` line 160 emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL` instead of a wiki-specific action. The `HandoverAction` enum (WorkflowEvent.kt:90) has no `COPY_WIKI` value. The override audit log therefore misidentifies wiki copies as email copies. However, the impact is confined to the 30-day override counter in Settings — it does not affect any functional behavior (the clipboard operation itself is correct), no data is lost, and users are unlikely to inspect the override log breakdown by action type. The fix (adding `COPY_WIKI` to the enum and updating line 160) is an additive, non-breaking change. It cannot break any passing tests; the `ShareTabTest` does not assert on the emitted action type.
recommendedAction: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in `WorkflowEvent.kt` and change `ShareTab.handleCopyWiki()` line 160 to emit `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI`.

title: HandoverState.currentBranch is a dead field — defined but never read
description: The `currentBranch: String = ""` field on `HandoverState` (line 9) is declared but never written to (no `copy(currentBranch = ...)` call anywhere in the module) and never read by any consumer (`ChecksTab`, `HandoverPanel`, `ShareTab`, `HandoverTicketHeader`, `HandoverPlaceholderResolver` — none reference it). It is also never set during `resetForNewTicket` or `resetStatusSlices`. It is a dead data field carrying zero information.
evidence: val currentBranch: String = "",  // line 9 — zero grep hits for reads in handover/src/main/
suggestedFix: Delete `val currentBranch: String = ""` from `HandoverState`. If branch context is needed in future, source it from `WorkflowContextService.state.value.activeBranch` (the canonical source) rather than a stale mirror on `HandoverState`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real dead field. `HandoverState.currentBranch: String = ""` (HandoverModels.kt line 9) has exactly zero read references anywhere in the handover module's src/main tree. A grep across the full handover/src/main tree yields only the declaration site. It is never written via `copy(currentBranch = ...)` in `HandoverStateService`, never read in `ChecksTab`, `HandoverPanel`, `ShareTab`, `HandoverTicketHeader`, or `HandoverPlaceholderResolver`. It is not reset in `resetForNewTicket` or `resetStatusSlices`. Deleting it has no behavioral impact; it cannot break any passing test.
recommendedAction: Delete `val currentBranch: String = ""` from `HandoverState`. Branch context should be sourced from `WorkflowContextService.state.value.activeBranch` if needed in the future.

title: JiraClosureService.project field is stored but never consumed
description: Lines 16-19 declare `private var project: Project? = null` and the DI constructor stores the `project` argument into it. Neither `buildClosureComment()` nor `mergeDockerTags()` — the only methods on the class — ever reference `this.project`. The field is write-only dead storage. The `@TestOnly` no-arg constructor leaves `project` null, confirming the service logic does not need it.
suggestedFix: Remove the `private var project: Project? = null` field and the `this.project = project` assignment from the DI constructor. The `Project` parameter in `constructor(project: Project)` and `companion.getInstance(project)` are correct IntelliJ DI boilerplate and must remain; only the stored field is dead.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real dead field. `JiraClosureService` stores `private var project: Project? = null` (line 16) and writes to it in the DI constructor (line 19). Neither `buildClosureComment()` nor `mergeDockerTags()` — the only non-companion methods — reference `this.project` or `project`. The `@TestOnly` no-arg constructor leaves `project` null, which is consistent with the service logic not needing it. The `Project` parameter in `constructor(project: Project)` and `companion.getInstance(project)` are correct IntelliJ service DI boilerplate and must remain. Only the stored field and the assignment `this.project = project` are dead. Removing them has no behavioral impact.
recommendedAction: Remove the `private var project: Project? = null` field declaration and the `this.project = project` assignment from the DI constructor. Keep the `constructor(project: Project)` signature and `companion.getInstance(project)` unchanged.

title: TimeTrackingService.hoursToSeconds() is dead in production — test-only callers
description: The public `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` (line 42) is called only from `TimeTrackingServiceTest` (lines 36-40). No production code in `:handover`, `:agent`, `:core`, or any other module calls it. `TimeLogCard.onLogClicked()` uses `hoursToJiraTimeString()` instead. The method was originally planned as a utility for `JiraService.logWork` but the design shifted to use the string form. A `docs/research/2026-05-07-handover-wireup-plan.md` note documents this transition.
evidence:
fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int = (hours * 3600).toInt()  // line 42
// Grep across entire worktree: only callers are TimeTrackingServiceTest lines 36-40
suggestedFix: Delete `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int = (hours * 3600).toInt()` from `TimeTrackingService` and delete the four test cases in `TimeTrackingServiceTest` that exercise it. No production callsite exists.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` at TimeTrackingService.kt:42 has exactly one caller: TimeTrackingServiceTest.kt lines 37-40. A worktree-wide grep finds no production callsite in any module. TimeLogCard.onLogClicked() (line 216) uses `hoursToJiraTimeString()` instead, which is the correct path per the Jira REST API requirement. Deleting the method and its four test assertions is safe — no production behavior changes.
recommendedAction: Delete `fun hoursToSeconds(hours: Double): Int` from TimeTrackingService.kt and remove the `hoursToSeconds converts correctly` test in TimeTrackingServiceTest.kt.

title: TimeTrackingService.formatStartedDate() and ISO_FORMAT are dead in production
description: `fun formatStartedDate(year, month, day, hour, minute)` (lines 64-70) and the companion `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87) are called only from `TimeTrackingServiceTest.formatStartedDate produces ISO 8601` — no production code calls them. `TimeLogCard.onLogClicked()` (line 221) constructs the `OffsetDateTime` directly via `LocalDateTime.of(...).atOffset(ZoneOffset.UTC)` (a comment there even references the former `formatStartedDate(.., 9, 0)` call that was replaced). The three imports `LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter` become dead if this method is removed.
suggestedFix: Delete `fun formatStartedDate(...)` (lines 64-70), `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87), and the three now-unused imports (`LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter`). Delete the corresponding test case `formatStartedDate produces ISO 8601`. Note: `JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856` has a code comment `"matches TimeTrackingService.ISO_FORMAT"` — update that comment to reference the local pattern string directly.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. `fun formatStartedDate(...)` at lines 64-70 and `private val ISO_FORMAT` at line 87 have no production callers. TimeLogCard.kt:220-223 constructs the `OffsetDateTime` inline (the comment on line 220 explicitly notes this replaced the `formatStartedDate` call). The only test caller is TimeTrackingServiceTest:44-48. JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856 references `TimeTrackingService.ISO_FORMAT` only in a KDoc comment, not in executable code — updating that comment is needed but not harmful. Deleting the method removes `LocalDateTime`, `ZoneOffset`, `DateTimeFormatter` imports from TimeTrackingService as well, since they are unused after removal.
recommendedAction: Delete `fun formatStartedDate(...)` (lines 64-70) and `private val ISO_FORMAT` (line 87) from TimeTrackingService.kt, remove the now-dead imports, delete the corresponding test, and update the KDoc in JiraServiceImpl.kt:1856 to reference the local pattern string.

title: HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.escape() duplicates core HtmlEscape.escapeHtml and is incomplete (misses single-quote)
description: Lines 325-326 define a private `escape(s: String)` inside the `HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer` object that replaces `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`. The listed core utility `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml()` (in `core/src/main/kotlin/com/workflow/orchestrator/core/util/HtmlEscape.kt`) performs identical escaping but also handles `'` → `'`. The `:handover` module already depends on `:core` (`implementation(project(":core"))`), so `HtmlEscape` is directly importable. This private reinvention (a) is flagged by the audit rule, (b) silently omits the apostrophe — a template author's name or ticket title containing `'` would render unescaped in the preview HTML.
suggestedFix: Delete the private `escape(s: String)` function and replace the three call sites (`escape(it)` in `applyInline`, `escape(it)` in the code block, `escape(it)` in the heading handler) with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml(it)`, adding `import com.workflow.orchestrator.core.util.HtmlEscape`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.escape() at lines 325-326 replaces `&`, `<`, `>`, `"` but omits `'` → `'`. HtmlEscape.escapeHtml() in core includes the apostrophe replacement and is importable from :handover (which already depends on :core). The missing apostrophe escape is a real gap: a template author name like `O'Brien` in a heading or table cell would render with a literal `'` in the HTML, which is technically valid in HTML5 body text but inconsistent with the core utility. The fix — replace the private `escape()` calls with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml()` — cannot break correct behavior since the core method is a superset.
recommendedAction: Delete the private `escape(s: String)` function and replace its three call sites in `applyInline` and the code-block handler with `HtmlEscape.escapeHtml(it)`, adding the appropriate import.

title: handlePostComment and handleCopyFormatted carry a dead @Suppress(UNUSED_PARAMETER) template param
description: Both `handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template)` (line 141) and `handleCopyFormatted(resolvedMarkup, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template)` (line 168) accept a `HandoverTemplate` second parameter they never use. The `@Suppress` is self-documenting dead weight. The template context is already fully applied before these methods are called (the caller resolves markup via `placeholderResolver`, so passing the raw template adds nothing). These are internal methods wired only from the `create()` lambdas at lines 280 and 294.
evidence:
internal suspend fun handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate) {  // line 141
internal suspend fun handleCopyFormatted(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate) {  // line 168
suggestedFix: Remove the `template: HandoverTemplate` parameter from `handlePostComment` and `handleCopyFormatted`. Update their lambda wrappers at lines 280 and 294 from `{ markup, tmpl -> holder[0]?.handlePostComment(markup, tmpl) }` to `{ markup, _ -> holder[0]?.handlePostComment(markup) }`. Also update the `@TestOnly` methods `testTriggerPostComment` and `testTriggerCopyFormatted` (lines 224, 229) accordingly.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. ShareTab.kt line 141: `handlePostComment(resolvedMarkup: String, @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") template: HandoverTemplate)` — the `template` parameter is never read inside the method body. Same at line 168 for `handleCopyFormatted`. The `@Suppress` annotation itself acknowledges the parameter is dead. The callers at the lambda wiring (lines 280 and 294) pass the template only to satisfy the signature. Removing the parameter requires updating the lambda signatures at those two call sites and the two `@TestOnly` methods at lines 224 and 229. This is a mechanical refactor with no behavioral change.
recommendedAction: Remove `template: HandoverTemplate` parameter from `handlePostComment` and `handleCopyFormatted`, update the lambda wrappers at lines 280/294, and update `testTriggerPostComment`/`testTriggerCopyFormatted` accordingly.

title: handleCopyWiki emits COPY_EMAIL override action — wrong telemetry for wiki-clipboard action
description: Line 160 of `handleCopyWiki` calls `emitOverrideIfNeeded(WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL)`. This is the wiki-markup-copy path (copies Jira wiki text to clipboard), not the HTML email path. Both `handleCopyWiki` (line 160) and `handleCopyFormatted` (line 169) emit `COPY_EMAIL`, making them indistinguishable in the `HandoverOverrideTracker` audit log and in `HandoverConfigurable`'s 30-day count. The `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`) has no `COPY_WIKI` variant, so the wiki-copy action is silently mislabeled.
suggestedFix: Add `COPY_WIKI` to `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction` in `core/src/main/kotlin/com/workflow/orchestrator/core/events/WorkflowEvent.kt`. Update `handleCopyWiki` (line 160) to emit `HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI` instead of `COPY_EMAIL`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. ShareTab.kt line 160: `handleCopyWiki` emits `WorkflowEvent.HandoverAction.COPY_EMAIL`, which is semantically wrong for a wiki-markup clipboard action. However, examining HandoverOverrideTracker.kt, the tracker records only timestamps (not action types) in `handoverOverrideLog` — so the audit log does not distinguish action types at all today. The `HandoverAction` enum (`POST_JIRA, COPY_EMAIL, FIX_COPYRIGHT, LOG_WORK, COPY_CHIP`) has no `COPY_WIKI` variant. The practical impact is limited to the `WorkflowEvent.HandoverOverride` payload being mislabeled; no downstream code routes on `COPY_EMAIL` vs. a hypothetical `COPY_WIKI`. The severity is Low, not the claimed Low — the finding is real but the actual observable impact in the current codebase is nil since the tracker ignores the action value.
recommendedAction: Add `COPY_WIKI` to the `HandoverAction` enum in WorkflowEvent.kt, then update `handleCopyWiki` at line 160 to emit `HandoverAction.COPY_WIKI`.

title: Stale KDoc comment in HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer — T10 was already delivered
description: Line 176 says `"T10 will wrap this with a project-scoped service that adds a live-render path."` but T10 is fully delivered — `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService` (the wrapping service) is defined in the same file (lines 29-167) immediately above the `HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer` object. The comment implies future work that is already done, which is misleading for maintainers.
suggestedFix: Replace the KDoc line with: `"Wrapped by [HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService] (above) which adds a live Jira render path."` or remove the sentence.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. HandoverWikiPreviewRenderer.kt line 176 reads `"T10 will wrap this with a project-scoped service that adds a live-render path."` but `HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService` is already fully defined in the same file at lines 29-167. The KDoc is misleading about future work that was already delivered. Fixing it is a trivial one-line KDoc update with zero behavioral impact.
recommendedAction: Replace the stale KDoc sentence with `"Wrapped by [HandoverWikiPreviewRendererService] (above) which provides the live Jira render path."` or remove it entirely.

title: CopyrightCellRenderer uses bare JLabel instead of JBLabel
description: CopyrightCellRenderer (lines 283–286) creates `JLabel(rel, icon, ...)` and `JLabel(suffix)` instead of `JBLabel`. The project convention requires JetBrains components for all UI elements. JLabel does not respect IDE HiDPI scaling, font scaling, or theme conventions the way JBLabel does. Both labels are user-visible (file path and status suffix in the copyright file list).
suggestedFix: Replace `JLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT)` with `JBLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT)` and `JLabel(suffix)` with `JBLabel(suffix)` in CopyrightCellRenderer. Add `import com.intellij.ui.components.JBLabel` and remove `import javax.swing.JLabel`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. CopyrightCellRenderer at lines 283 and 286 creates JLabel instances. The project convention requires JBLabel for all UI elements (per CLAUDE.md and the finding). The file already imports JBLabel (line 13 imports com.intellij.ui.components.JBLabel) so the fix is purely to change the two instantiation call sites. JLabel is still imported at line 39 (javax.swing.JLabel) but that import becomes unused after the fix, which is minor. The fix is trivially safe — JBLabel is a drop-in subclass of JLabel that honors IDE scaling and theming.
recommendedAction: In CopyrightCellRenderer, change JLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT) to JBLabel(rel, icon, SwingConstants.LEFT) at line 283, and JLabel(suffix) to JBLabel(suffix) at line 286. Remove the now-unused javax.swing.JLabel import.
